expectations.text: Advises against changing things while progress is still occurring
expectations.evidence: 'If a program is working—meaning you're making progress, staying healthy, and recovering adequately—the default move is to iterate and refine, not abandon it.' Lists 'Green flags that suggest tweaking will work.'

expectations.text: When plateau appears, recommends deciding whether the lift needs more stress or less stress
expectations.evidence: Step 2 'Diagnose the Limiting Factor' covers both directions: Volume/Frequency Mismatch (possibly too much stress) vs Weak Link in the Chain (possibly too little targeted stress). Section B suggests reducing deadlift volume/frequency when overtrained.

expectations.text: Recommends small, targeted changes (volume, intensity, frequency, or exercise selection) evaluated individually over weeks
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'Make ONE Change at a Time. Critical rule: Change one variable, measure for 4–6 weeks, then decide.' Priority order: technique → volume/frequency → accessories → program structure.

expectations.text: Only switch programs entirely after serial targeted tweaks have failed
expectations.evidence: Step 5: 'Only switch if: You've diagnosed the limiting factor, You've made targeted tweaks and given them 6–12 weeks, You're still not progressing.' Don't switch after just one tweak.
